data.title: Keppel’s Bifrost subsea cable achieves 'ready for service' status
data.description:
It is the first to connect Singapore directly to the U.S. west coast via Indonesia.

Keppel Ltd. has announced that the Bifrost Cable System has achieved ready for service (RFS) status and will begin carrying commercial traffic in the coming weeks.

The 20,000-km subsea cable is the first to connect Singapore directly to the U.S. west coast via Indonesia.

It offers a round-trip latency of under 165 milliseconds between Singapore and the U.S., up to 10 milliseconds faster than existing systems, and adds over 260 Tbps of capacity to one of the world’s busiest digital corridors.

Keppel has been allocated five out of the system’s 12 fibre pairs through a 40-60 joint venture with private fund co-investors. The cable lands in Singapore, Guam, and Grover Beach, California, with branching connections to Jakarta and Manado in Indonesia, Davao in the Philippines, and Winema, Oregon.

Converge ICT will manage the landing in Davao, Philippines.

Construction of the Bifrost system was carried out by Alcatel Submarine Networks (ASN).

data.title: STI poised to maintain year-end target at 4,430
data.description:
An oversold market breadth sets the market for a potential year-end rally.

The Singapore Times Index is poised to maintain its year-end target of 4,430, underpinned by MAS equity market support measures and attractive forward dividend yield, according to a DBS report.

The report also said that an oversold market breadth sets the market for a potential year-end rally, with small-mid-caps (SMCs) to benefit from further MAS support measures.

“Any positive progress in US-China trade talks before the 10 November deadline could catalyse regional market rallies,” DBS said.

Moreover, an eventful fourth quarter awaits small-mid-cap stocks, with the recent pullback in SMCs presenting an opportunity to add ahead of anticipated Q4 momentum.

“We find value plays beyond simply low-valuation stocks, focusing instead on stocks with value-unlocking potential, quality stocks with low visibility, and those with scope to improve returns through active capital management,” DBS added.

Further, industrial and tech names anticipated to deliver growth rates above the sector average include SIA Eng, SATS, Yangzijiang, and Seatrium.

data.title: ESR exits Kenedix investment
data.description:
The group said this is to simplify the business and focus on its core strengths.

ESR has fully realised its investment in Kenedix, Inc. as part of its strategy to simplify the business and focus on its core strengths.

SMFL MIRAI Partners, a wholly owned subsidiary of Sumitomo Mitsui Finance and Leasing, has acquired ESR’s 30% stake in the Japanese real estate asset management company, giving the SMFL Group 100% ownership of Kenedix.

This follows ESR’s sale of the ARA Private Funds business to SMFL MIRAI Partners and Kenedix in 2024.

data.title: Keppel DC REIT launches $404.5m preferential offering
data.description:
It will issue 180.6 million new units at $2.24 each.

Keppel DC REIT will raise $404.5m through a non-renounceable preferential offering of 180,562,518 new units at $2.24 per unit.

Unitholders will be entitled to subscribe for 80 New Units for every 1,000 existing units held as at the record date of 30 September 2025, 5.00 p.m. Fractions of units will be disregarded.

The offering opens on 3 October 2025, 9.00 a.m. and closes on 13 October 2025.

Applications can be made via ATMs of participating banks, the SGX Investor Portal (PayNow), or by submitting the ARE form to CDP.

CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS), Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS) investors, and those holding units through finance companies or depository agents must apply through their respective intermediaries.

Keppel DC Investment Holdings (17.98% stake) and Keppel DC REIT Management (1.10% stake) have both committed to subscribe in full for their entitlements.

Trading of the new units on SGX-ST is expected to begin on 22 October 2025, 9.00 a.m.

data.title: Avation posts $7.7m loss in FY2025
data.description:
The group’s revenue rose 19.2% to $110.1m.

Avation PLC reported a loss after tax of $7.7m for the year ended 30 June 2025, compared with a $19.7m profit last year.

The group’s revenue rose 19.2% to $110.1m. The company declared a dividend of 1.0 US cent per share.

Executive Chairman Jeff Chatfield said that all of its aircraft were on lease throughout the year. He highlighted several milestones, including the transition of an ATR 72-600 to Colombian airline Clic Air, an extension of an Airbus A320-200 lease with easyJet, and the signing of leases for the first two aircraft from its ten-unit ATR orderbook.

Avation also added Etihad Airways as a new customer following the purchase of an Airbus A320-200, while two ATR 72-600s were sold to existing lessees exercising purchase options.

In September, Avation sold a Boeing 777-300ER at a profit above book value. The proceeds are being used to reduce debt and reinvest in narrowbody aircraft.

The company reduced its net debt to assets ratio to 54.8% and cut outstanding 2026 notes to $298 million.

Moody’s and Fitch assigned first-time ratings of B1/B2 and B, respectively, both with stable outlooks.

Avation said it plans to refinance its 2026 notes and expand its fleet through new ATR 72-600 deliveries.

data.title: First Resources sells West Papua subsidiaries
data.description:
The subsidiaries had debts of $103.3m.

First Resources Limited has sold its indirect subsidiaries PT Permata Putera Mandiri (PT PPM) and PT Putera Manunggal Perkasa (PT PMP) for $32,200 (IDR 405.6m).

The disposal follows the company’s acquisition of PT Austindo Nusantara Jaya Tbk (PT ANJ) in May 2025 and its decision to exit the oil palm plantation business in Indonesia’s West Papua province.

The two subsidiaries held around 7,400 hectares of oil palm plantations, a crude palm oil mill, and unplanted land bank.

As of 31 July 2025, they carried net financial debt of $103.3m (IDR 1.31t).

After the sale, PT PPM and PT PMP are no longer subsidiaries of the group.

First Resources said the transaction is not expected to materially affect its net tangible assets or earnings per share for the year ending 31 December 2025.

The disposals are classified as non-discloseable transactions under Singapore Exchange rules.

data.title: GS Holdings incorporates new subsidiary
data.description:
Principal activities include the management and licensing of intellectual property. 

GS Holdings Limited has incorporated Tomorrow Brands Pte. Ltd. as a wholly-owned subsidiary on 2 October 2025.

Principal activities include the management and licensing of intellectual property, such as brand names and trademarks.

The incorporation was undertaken through internal resources and will not have any material impact on the company’s financial year ending 31 March 2026.

data.title: COTD: Prime retail rents up by 0.3% QoQ in Q3
data.description:
Suburban prime malls also saw rents grow 0.3% QoQ.

Prime retail rents rose by 0.3% quarter on quarter during the third quarter of the year as international brands seek to gain a foothold in high-footfall locations, according to a Cushman & Wakefield report.

Recent new-to-market entrants include Australia’s Yo-Chi, China’s Joocyee at Wisma Atria, Denmark’s Flying Tiger Copenhagen, and US’ Alo.

Suburban prime malls also saw rents grow 0.3% QoQ, driven by resilient non-discretionary spending and low vacancy rates across all suburban retail spaces.

Despite closures amongst some F&B and cinema operators, other activity-based retailers are still expanding.

data.title: UOL completes Thomson View condo sale
data.description:
The development houses 200 apartments.

UOL Group Limited has completed the $810m sale of the Thomson View condominium, according to a bourse filing.

The development houses 200 apartments, 54 townhouses and a shop unit on a 5-hectare site.

Its price makes it the largest en bloc deal done in Singapore since Chuan Park’s $890m sale in May 2023.

data.title: Commercial site at Changi Road up for sale for $38m
data.description:
The site has a total strata area of approximately 13,520 square feet.

A freehold commercial development prominently comprising 21 strata-titled shops at Changi Road is up for sale for $38m via Expression of Interest, according to AlpsEdge Real Estate and Brilliance Capital.

The site has a total strata area of approximately 13,520 square feet (sq. ft.) with unit sizes ranging from 388 sq. ft. to 904 sq. ft.

It comprises 73 units in total, with three levels of retail and three levels of offices, supported by a basement car park with 25 lots and two loading bays.

The site is predominantly tenanted, providing investors with immediate rental income and stability. Except for a recently vacated second-storey unit, all other units are leased.
